zh.Henlow, , Bedfordshire, England: 英國英格蘭貝德福爾亨洛福
zh.lifeSummary: 當Robert Tilley於1540年5月出生在英國貝德福爾亨洛福時，他的父親William Tilley25歲，母親Agnes Nightengale Cowper21歲。他於1564年8月14日與Elizabeth在英國英格蘭貝德福爾亨洛福聖 馬也斯 邱奇結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和4個女兒的父母。他去世於1613年2月在他的家鄉享年72歲，埋葬在英國貝德福爾亨洛福。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Robert Tilley于1540年5月出生在英国貝德福爾亨洛福时，他的父亲William Tilley25岁，母亲Agnes Nightengale Cowper21岁。他于1564年8月14日与Elizabeth在英国英格兰貝德福爾亨洛福聖 馬也斯 邱奇结婚。他们至少是4个儿子和4个女儿的父母。他去世于1613年2月在他的家乡享年72岁，埋葬在英国貝德福爾亨洛福。

nameMeanings.name: Tilley
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (of Norman origin): habitational name from Tilly-sur-Seulles (Calvados) or Tilly (Eure), France.

English: habitational name from Tyley in Wotton under Edge (Gloucestershire), Tiley in Minterne Magna (Dorset), or Tilley (Shropshire). The Gloucestershire and Dorset placenames derive from Old English tigel ‘tile’ + lēah ‘wood, woodland clearing’. The Shropshire placename derives from an uncertain first element + Old English lēah.
English: occupational name from Middle English tilie ‘one who tills the land, a husbandman’. Compare Tiller .

variants.name: Tillie
meanings: English (northern England and southern Scotland): variant of   Tilley  . This form of the surname is rare in Britain and Ireland.

variants.name: Tiller
meanings: English (southern, especially Hampshire): occupational name from Middle English tiliere, tilyer ‘one who tills the soil, husbandman, farmer, farm laborer’. This is a derivative of Old English tilian ‘to till (the soil)’, Middle English tilie. There has been some confusion of this name with Tyler .

variants.name: Tiley
meanings: English (Gloucestershire, Somerset and Wiltshire): variant of   Tilley  .

nameMeanings.name: Robert
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the many French names of Germanic origin that were introduced into Britain by the Normans; it has since remained in continuous use. It is derived from the nearly synonymous elements hrōd ‘fame’ + berht ‘bright, famous’, and had a native Old English predecessor of similar form (Hreodbeorht), which was supplanted by the Norman name. Two dukes of Normandy in the 11th century bore the name: the father of William the Conqueror (sometimes identified with the legendary Robert the Devil), and his eldest son. It was borne also by three kings of Scotland, notably Robert the Bruce ( 1274–1329 ), who freed Scotland from English domination. The altered short form Bob is very common, but Hob and Dob, which were common in the Middle Ages and gave rise to surnames, are extinct. See also Rupert .

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The Royal Exchange is Organized
historicEvents.description: The Royal Exchange was organized in January 1571 by Sir Thomas Gresham. Gresham is known as the father of English banking.

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

historicEvents.title: Elizabeth I Signs Death Warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots
historicEvents.description: Elizabeth I signed the death warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots, on February 1, 1587, as she was found guilty of plotting to assasinate Elizabeth. Mary, Queen of Scots, was beheaded at Fotheringhay Castle in England on February 8, 1587.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Elizabeth I.org - Mary Queen of Scots

historicEvents.title: The Honourable East India Company
historicEvents.description: The Honourable East India Company, a British joint-stock company, was established in 1600 for trade in the Indian Ocean region. At its height, the British East India Company had a private army which was twice the size of the British Army, ruled large sections of India, and revenues in the millions.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Historic UK - The East India Company

en.lifeSummary: When Robert Tilley was born in May 1540, in Henlow, Bedfordshire, England, his father, William Tilley, was 25 and his mother, Agnes Nightengale Cowper, was 21. He married Elizabeth on 14 August 1564, in St Mary's Church, Henlow, Bedfordshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 4 daughters. He died in February 1613, in his hometown, at the age of 72, and was buried in Henlow, Bedfordshire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Robert Tilley est né en mai 1540, à Henlow, Bedfordshire, Angleterre, son père, William Tilley, avait 25 ans et sa mère, Agnes Nightengale Cowper, avait 21 ans. Il a épousé Elizabeth le 14 août 1564, à St Mary's Church, Henlow, Bedfordshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 4 filles. Il est décédé en février 1613, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 72 ans, et a été enterré à Henlow, Bedfordshire, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Robert Tilley im Mai 1540 in Henlow, Bedfordshire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater William Tilley 25 und seine Mutter Agnes Nightengale Cowper 21. Er hat am 14. August 1564 in St Mary's Church, Henlow, Bedfordshire, England, United Kingdom Elizabeth geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne und 4 Töchter. Er starb im Februar 1613 im Alter von 72 Jahren in seinem Geburtsort und wurde in Henlow, Bedfordshire, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Robert Tilley è nato il nel maggio 1540 nel Henlow, Bedfordshire, England, il suo padre, William Tilley, aveva 25 anni e il suo madre, Agnes Nightengale Cowper, aveva 21 anni. Lui sposato Elizabeth il 14 agosto 1564, nel St Mary's Church, Henlow, Bedfordshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 4 figli e 4 figlie. Morì nel febbraio 1613, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 72 anni, e fu sepolto a Henlow, Bedfordshire, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Robert Tilleyが1540年5月にイギリスベドフォードシュアイアヘンロウで生まれた時，父William Tilleyは25歳，母Agnes Nightengale Cowperは21歳であった。1564年8月14日にイギリスイギリスベドフォードシュアイアヘンロウスト マリ'ス チャーチでElizabethと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子と4人の娘の親であった。彼は1613年2月に故郷で72歳で死去し，イギリスベドフォードシュアイアヘンロウにて埋葬された。

ko.St Mary's Church, Henlow, Bedfordshire,: 영국 잉글랜드 베드퍼드실르 헨로 스트 마이'스 c후르치
ko.lifeSummary: Robert Tilley(이)가 1540년 5월 영국 베드퍼드실르 헨로에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 William Tilley(은)는 25세였고, 그의 어머니 Agnes Nightengale Cowper(은)는 21세였다. 그는 1564년 8월 14일 영국 잉글랜드 베드퍼드실르 헨로 스트 마이'스 c후르치에서 Elizabeth(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1613년 2월 그의 고향에서 72세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 베드퍼드실르 헨로에 묻혔다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Robert Tilley родился май 1540 г. в местности хенлоу, бедфордшир, Англия, его отцу по имени William Tilley было 25 лет, а матери, которую звали Agnes Nightengale Cowper, 21. Он женился на женщине по имени Elizabeth 14 августа 1564 г. в местности ст мэри'с чурч, хенлоу, бедфордшир, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 4 дочери. Он умер февраль 1613 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 72 лет и был похоронен в местности хенлоу, бедфордшир, Англия.
